div.h2: ptrace Tracing Lifecycle — Fork → TRACEME → Exec → SIGTRAP → Loop

g.text: MAIN TRACING LOOP (repeats until child exits)
Observer Effect — why tracing slows programs 10x
untraced getpid() | ~100 ns | vDSO fast path, no ring-0 switch
ptrace entry context sw | ~1-10 µs | CR3 load, TLB pressure, PCB save
ptrace exit context sw | ~1-10 µs | CR3 restore, TLB partial flush
total per syscall | ~2-20 µs | 20-200x actual syscall cost
PTRACE_O_TRACESYSGOOD | 0x01 — set bit7 on syscall stops
copy_mm (COW) | dup page table, mark read-only
copy fd table | share open file descriptors
set TASK_RUNNING | child ready to schedule
flush old address space | unmap all vmas
detect PT_PTRACED flag | tracee has tracer
send SIGTRAP to child | stops before 1st instruction
child → TASK_RUNNING | added to run queue
child hits syscall | trap into kernel
parent becomes tracer | kernel records relationship
must call before exec | after exec: code is replaced

text.tspan: execvp: searches PATH — use for tracing named programs

g.text: RIP | entry point of new program
rax | same as orig_rax at entry
NOTE | kernel has NOT executed syscall yet
rax | return value (may be negative errno)
rdi-r9 | may be clobbered by kernel
WHY -4095 | valid negatives (mmap addrs) outside this range
SIGTERM suppressed | program never exits cleanly
SIGCHLD suppressed | parent never reaps children
SIGPIPE suppressed | writes to closed pipes hang
